text: The need for regulations in the event of threats to the functioning and in particular to the existence of the state is met in each country’s law through specific regulations on states of emergency. The obligation to secure the state and its citizens is largely borne by state administrative organs. Regulations concerning the event of an emergency should not give way to any interpretation as to the liability of these bodies in this respect. A state of emergency can only be introduced in the case of the failure of normal constitutional means to function. The purpose of this article is to reflect on and present the evolution of the state of emergency institution in the newly restored Polish state, based on the March Constitution of 17th of March 1921 and the April Constitution of 23rd of April 1935, as well as other acts that were adopted during this period and which, due to their importance, influenced the regulation of the state’s security. In the period under consideration, the adoption of special legal regulations for given situations was due to the current internal or external situation of the state. The article was written using the dogmatic-legal and historical-legal methods of research.
